quiz.title: Grade-10 Physics: Unit 10 Quiz 9
quiz.subtitle: Revision: Kinematics, Forces, Energy, and Work
quiz.subject: Physics

questions.question: A 1500 kg car accelerates uniformly from rest and covers 100 m in 8.0 seconds. What is the net force acting on the car?
questions.explanation: First, find acceleration using s = ut + ½at². With u = 0, s = 100 m, t = 8.0 s: 100 = ½ × a × 64, so a = 3.125 m/s². Then apply Newton's second law: F = ma = 1500 × 3.125 = 4688 N.

questions.question: A force of 200 N pushes a 50 kg crate across a frictionless floor for 6.0 seconds, starting from rest. What is the kinetic energy of the crate at the end of the 6.0 seconds?
questions.explanation: Find acceleration: a = F/m = 200/50 = 4 m/s². Find final velocity: v = u + at = 0 + 4 × 6 = 24 m/s. Kinetic energy: KE = ½mv² = ½ × 50 × (24)² = ½ × 50 × 576 = 14400 J.

questions.question: A 2.0 kg ball is dropped from a height of 45 m. Ignoring air resistance, what is its velocity just before hitting the ground? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Using v² = u² + 2as: v² = 0 + 2 × 10 × 45 = 900, so v = 30 m/s. Alternatively, time to fall: t = √(2h/g) = √(90/10) = 3 s, then v = gt = 10 × 3 = 30 m/s.

questions.question: A 500 N force is applied to a crate at an angle of 30° above the horizontal. The crate moves 8.0 m horizontally across a frictionless surface. How much work is done by the force?
questions.explanation: Only the horizontal component of the force does work. F_horizontal = 500 × cos30° = 500 × 0.866 = 433 N. Work = F_horizontal × distance = 433 × 8.0 = 3464 J.

questions.question: A motor lifts a 300 kg mass vertically upward at a constant speed of 2.0 m/s. What is the power output of the motor? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: At constant speed, the motor's force equals the weight: F = mg = 300 × 10 = 3000 N. Power = force × velocity = 3000 × 2.0 = 6000 W.

questions.question: A 0.50 kg ball is thrown vertically upward with an initial velocity of 20 m/s. What maximum height does it reach? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: At maximum height, v = 0. Using v² = u² + 2as: 0 = 400 + 2(-10)s, so 20s = 400, s = 20 m. Alternatively, using energy conservation: ½mv² = mgh, ½ × 0.5 × 400 = 0.5 × 10 × h, so 100 = 5h, h = 20 m.

questions.question: A 1200 kg car is moving at 15 m/s when the driver applies the brakes. The braking force is 4500 N. What is the braking distance?
questions.explanation: Using work-energy: KE = ½mv² = ½ × 1200 × 225 = 135000 J. Work done by brakes = F × d, so d = KE/F = 135000/4500 = 30 m. Alternatively, a = F/m = 3.75 m/s², then v² = u² + 2as: 0 = 225 + 2(-3.75)s, s = 225/7.5 = 30 m.

questions.question: A 4.0 kg box slides down a frictionless incline. It starts from rest and reaches the bottom with a speed of 10 m/s. What is the vertical height of the incline? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Energy conservation: potential energy at top = kinetic energy at bottom. mgh = ½mv². Mass cancels: 10 × h = ½ × 100, so 10h = 50, h = 5.0 m.

questions.question: The velocity-time graph of an object shows a straight line from (0 s, 0 m/s) to (10 s, 20 m/s). What is the displacement of the object during these 10 seconds?
questions.explanation: Displacement equals the area under the velocity-time graph. The graph forms a triangle: area = ½ × base × height = ½ × 10 × 20 = 100 m.

questions.question: A 60 kg student climbs a flight of stairs rising 5.0 m vertically in 12 seconds. What is the student's average power output? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Work done = mgh = 60 × 10 × 5 = 3000 J. Power = work/time = 3000/12 = 250 W.

questions.question: A 250 g ball is kicked horizontally off a 20 m high cliff at 15 m/s. How far from the base of the cliff does the ball land? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: Time to fall is determined by vertical motion: h = ½gt², so 20 = ½ × 10 × t², t² = 4, t = 2 s. Horizontal distance = horizontal velocity × time = 15 × 2 = 30 m. Mass does not affect the result.

questions.question: A net force of 2400 N acts on an 800 kg car for 5.0 seconds, starting from rest. What is the work done by the force?
questions.explanation: Find acceleration: a = F/m = 2400/800 = 3 m/s². Distance: s = ut + ½at² = 0 + ½ × 3 × 25 = 37.5 m. Work = F × d = 2400 × 37.5 = 90000 J. Alternatively, final velocity v = at = 3 × 5 = 15 m/s, then work = change in KE = ½ × 800 × (15)² = 90000 J.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered kinematics, forces, energy, and work calculations!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review multi-step problems and check which formula to apply in each situation.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on using the correct formula and converting units where needed.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: equations of motion, Newton's second law, work-energy theorem, and power calculations.
